facts.statement: Citigroup Inc. is identified in SEC submissions as a National Commercial Bank.
Citigroup's SEC submissions metadata lists NYSE as its exchange.
Citigroup reported FY2025 revenues of $78.066 billion.
Citigroup reported FY2025 net income of $9.228 billion.
Citigroup reported FY2025 operating cash flow of negative $73.416 billion.
Citigroup reported stockholders' equity of $208.598 billion at FY2025.
Citigroup reported long-term debt of $287.3 billion at FY2025.
Citigroup reported cash and cash equivalents of $133.097 billion for FY2018 in the company facts summary.
Citigroup's 10-K lists operating segments including Services, Markets, Banking, Wealth, and US Personal Banking.
Citigroup's 10-K glossary defines its International region as comprising six clusters: United Kingdom; Japan, Asia North and Australia; LATAM; Asia South; Europe; and Middle East, Africa and Russia.
Citigroup's 10-K glossary describes Branded Cards as a business with proprietary cards, co-branded cards, and personal installment loans.
Citigroup's 10-K glossary describes Retail Services as its U.S. retail services cards business with co-brand and private label relationships, including The Home Depot, Best Buy, and Macy's.

Citigroup's 10-K glossary says Treasury and Trade Solutions provides cash management and trade-related services; the glossary also defines commercial cards as payment services for corporate and public sector clients, including procurement, corporate travel and entertainment, expense management, and business-to-business payment solutions.

Citigroup's 10-K glossary defines net new investment asset flows for Wealth as investment asset inflows less outflows, excluding fees, commissions, market movement, internal transfers, and strategic exits.

Citigroup's 10-K glossary states that in October 2020 Citigroup and Citibank entered consent orders with the FRB and OCC requiring improvements in enterprise-wide risk management, compliance, data quality management related to governance, and internal controls; it also states that in July 2024 the FRB and OCC entered civil money penalty consent orders to address remediation shortcomings.
Citigroup's 10-K glossary says the company has embarked on a multiyear transformation aimed at strengthening risk and controls while improving value to customers, clients, and shareholders.

The 2026 proxy statement says Citigroup continued simplifying the franchise through exits of non-core businesses outside the United States, including progress on Banamex, agreement to sell its consumer business in Poland, and completion of the sale of AO Citibank in Russia in February 2026.

The 2026 proxy statement says Jane Fraser is Chair of the Board and Chief Executive Officer, John Dugan is Lead Independent Director, Mark A. L. Mason is Chief Financial Officer, and Brent J. McIntosh is Chief Legal Officer and Corporate Secretary.
Citigroup's 2026 Annual Meeting materials state that stockholders are being asked to elect 13 directors.
The 2026 Annual Meeting materials include an advisory vote to approve 2025 executive compensation and a proposal to approve additional shares for the Citigroup 2019 Stock Incentive Plan.
The 2026 proxy statement says Citigroup returned over $17.5 billion to stockholders in 2025, including $13.250 billion of common stock repurchases and $4.340 billion of common dividends.
The 2026 proxy statement says Citigroup ended 2025 with a CET1 capital ratio of 13.2%, roughly 160 basis points above its minimum regulatory requirement.
The 2026 proxy statement says Citigroup reported 2025 RoTCE of 8.8% excluding notable items.
The 2026 proxy statement says 2025 results included notable items related to Russia HFS accounting treatment loss impact and a Mexico goodwill impairment charge.
